section.heading: Transfer to governmental or other legal entity; determination of transferee

subsection.chapeau: Transfer may be made to one or more of the following, if the transferee has the legal authority to receive and operate the utility.

paragraph.content: the State in which the community is located;
any political subdivision or agency of that State; or
any person, firm, corporation, or other legal entity.

subsection.chapeau: In determining the transferee for any utility, the Commission may consider the following:

paragraph.content: the pattern of ownership of the comparable utilities in the State in which the community is located;
the ability of the transferee to operate the utility;
the probable price of the sale of the utility, the ability of the transferee to pay that price, and any probable expense;
the desires of the eligible voters of the community as directly expressed in any vote in any officially recognized procedure or in any procedure established by the Commission; and
the benefit to the United States in reducing possible requirements for local assistance as authorized in subchapters VII and VIII of this chapter.

note.heading: Transfer of Functions
note.p: Atomic Energy Commission abolished and functions transferred by sections 5814 and 5841 of this title. See also Transfer of Functions notes set out under those sections.
